title: The Greek mystery rituals with Brian Muraresku
summary: Brian Muraresku discusses ancient Greek mystery rituals and altered states of consciousness, examining how pre-Socratic philosophers like Parmenides, Heraclitus, and Pythagoras practiced incubation rites in underground temples. The conversation explores how these practices influenced Plato's philosophy about death and transcendence.

key_claims.claim: Plato's philosophy was focused on the practicing of death and being dead
Pre-Socratic philosophers practiced rites of incubation in underground temples to meet a goddess and enter altered states of consciousness
During incubation rites, practitioners would tread between life and death with barely detectable pulse
